Bizzy Bone's major label comeback through After Platinum Records, distributed via Virgin Records. Heralded by critics as his best album in years, featuring appearances from DMX, Joel Madden of Good Charlotte, Jim Jones, and Twista. A mature and polished effort that showcased Bizzy's versatility across genres.
And when I feel so dam dirty dirty, By leaning on a leer jet, Baby ain't here yet, Henny, Caffeine, & nicotine, Notice I left out the green.

A fascinating and cryptic album that marked a significant shift in Bizzy Bone's musical and lyrical approach. Fans and critics responded positively to its spiritual depth and lo-fi production. The title references the biblical concept of God as the beginning and the end, a recurring theme throughout Bizzy's solo work. A masterpiece featuring guest appearances from members of Bizzy Bone’s group 7th Sign Regime. On “The Streets,” Bizzy’s 7th sign affiliate, best friend and foster brother Capo raps, “Twenty-two years and still counting…”, but he was tragically shot and killed before the album’s release. Bizzy later said: “After that happened I was thinking about quitting music, quitting rapping and singing and the whole thing. It went deep. It kinda messed me up for a while. But we go on, we got no choice.”
Evidently this is real, but it don't barely feel, Let's do this studio grimey, quick 'fore I get killed

Art of War: World War III

The ninth studio album by Bone Thugs-N-Harmony and their sequel to the legendary quadruple-platinum Art of War. Features all five original members despite internal conflict. Krayzie and Wish only appear via previously unreleased tracks recorded for Uni5. Produced primarily by DJ U-Neek.
High enough to be social, zone out wit' a glass of Bourbon, I'm pearling, swerving on the freeway, purposely, close curtain.

Released just months after Bone Thugs-N-Harmony reunited all five original members in late 2008, Bizzy Bone channeled that renewed energy into this solo album. The title refers to Bizzy Bone’s return to Hi Power Entertainment following Thugs Revenge, released three years earlier. Snoop Dogg is credited as a guest on “Let’s Get High,” but only delivers a single line.
Khaki's freshly creased like I'm fresh out the county, howdy.

Layzie Bone, Wish Bone & Krayzie Bone

The direct follow-up to Back with the Thugz from just four months earlier. Bizzy had recorded enough material for two albums. Widely considered the stronger of the two volumes, with better production and more focused performances. Produced by Seriouss Sam, Makavelik, and Nu-Ration, though the album packaging does not credit producers for individual tracks.
Lets go through the worm hole, and I stand, as if sand, I demand much respect, David, God has a plan.

The second installment in the Armageddon trilogy with Bizzy Bone and AC Killer, released on Christmas Day 2015. Among the Bone-affiliated artists featured on the album is The Notorious B.I.G., who appears on the track ‘Death Is Calling‘ through an archived verse from ‘Suicidal Thoughts‘. As with the first installment, the album is entirely produced by Heinz.
We are Cooley High, Macho Camacho, Bring on that Cobra Kai, Capo to capo.

Released on his 48th birthday, September 12, 2024, this self-titled statement is one of his most personal releases to date. Entirely self-produced with no guest features, the physical CD ships bundled with an official lyric book, a rare inclusion that signals Bizzy's view of himself first and foremost as a poet and wordsmith. Lyrically the album reaches across decades and themes: '97' Fed Cases' revisits the legal turmoil of his early career, while '#PrayforKray' is a direct tribute to Krayzie Bone, who nearly died in September 2023 following emergency surgery for a bleeding artery in his lungs. Across 14 tracks the album moves through spirituality, racial identity, social commentary, and his lifelong fascination with the paranormal.
Go live on Instagram, Pull out the musket!

Bizzy Bone and Layzie Bone reunite after going separate ways, delivering a celebration of brotherhood and Midwest loyalty. A classic within the Bone Thugs-N-Harmony catalog, also featuring appearances from Krayzie Bone and Wish Bone. Peaked at #60 on the Billboard 200 and #3 on Independent Albums.
Puttin' a pistol to my brain, one second before I squeeze since I'm a gangster, I'm a put myself on my knees.

The second installment of the Bone Brothers series by Bizzy Bone and Layzie Bone. Released through Real Talk Entertainment, the album continues the duo's celebration of Cleveland street life, brotherhood and loyalty. Rather than new recordings, it consists of reworked material from earlier solo releases on the same label.
They better remember that to the coldest winters pimp hand, Slippin ain't never an option, I get the trippin, Flip when they dip when they skippin.

The third and most acclaimed Bone Brothers album by Bizzy Bone and Layzie Bone, widely considered the best of the trilogy. Unlike Vol. 2 which recycled earlier verses, this is all new material with stronger production from Batkave, Jaz, Thin C, Steve Vicious and Cricket. The track “Cash Money” features The Notorious B.I.G., reusing his original verse from “The What,” which he later rewrote before its official release on his debut album “Ready to Die“.
I went through the drama and caught up in all of the lies, whatever was on my side, Christ provided my body, you told me I'm Bryon, and this is your life.

The fourth entry in the Bone Brothers series. Like its predecessor, it was released through Siccness.net, but unlike the previous installment, it does not feature new material, instead relying on reworked tracks from earlier releases on the label. Fun fact: The album artwork credits every track as “performed by Layzie Bizzy and Bizzy Bone”, a misprint that reflects the rushed nature of the project.
Dear father you know that I love you, tell me how was we born? In a Matrix, in a maze, or a haze or a storm?

The fourth studio album by Bone Thugs-N-Harmony, debuting at #2 on the Billboard 200 (#1 R&B) and quickly went Platinum. Notably features Flesh-n-Bone on 14 of 15 tracks, his last appearance before a decade-long prison sentence. Production was handled by DJ U-Neek, L.T. Hutton, Jimmy Thomas and Darren Vegas. A darker, more introspective record that marked the group's return after a three-year gap.
Move faster and then ask 'em about the state of men and help the matter, Brace yourself, I can't tell if I'm in peril.

The first release in Bizzy's annual September 12 birthday tradition. Widely regarded by fans as a genuine late-career highlight, this album marked a creative resurgence that many had stopped expecting. Produced entirely by Blais, whose beats fuse classic 90s Midwest atmospherics with something distinctly contemporary, it showcases Bizzy at his most focused and lyrically sharp in years. The album carries a confrontational edge rooted in a very real beef: after Migos publicly claimed to be the greatest group of all time, Bizzy took it personally and the title track became a direct response, delivering the kind of complex, aggressive lyricism that defined his peak years. That combative energy ripples through much of the album, giving it an urgency that sets it apart from his more introspective work. A limited Gold Edition of 1,000 signed copies was pressed, bundled with a t-shirt and poster Discogs, making it one of the more collectible items in his catalog.
B-G, I'm a knockie fan, I hit you wit' one of these combinations, Have you goin’ through complications, And then I do my Tekashi dance.
All We Got Is Each Other

The album is the first chapter in the Armageddon trilogy, released through CNO Records. AC Killer first met Bizzy Bone at an autograph signing at a record store in Los Angeles in 2007. Bizzy felt that AC reminded him of his brother, the late Capo. They quickly developed strong chemistry, and the very next day AC brought his studio equipment to Bizzy’s hotel, where they recorded their first song together. The album is fully produced by CNO in-house producer Heinz and features appearances from Bone and Mo Thugs affiliates.
Get me the paper, Let me read while I hold my pistol.

The debut EP that announced Bone Thugs-n-Harmony to the world, released on Ruthless Records after Eazy-E had signed the group and helped shape their new name. Featuring their mentor as executive producer, it remains the only project the group released during his lifetime. Singles 'Thuggish Ruggish Bone' and 'Foe tha Love of $' established the template that would define their career, rapid-fire melodic flows delivered in tight vocal harmony over DJ U-Neek's dark, hypnotic production. The EP is laced with small details that reward close listening, including an intro whose opening lines are recorded backwards, and producer credits hidden in plain sight as 'Keenu Songs,' spelling U-Neek in reverse. Selected as one of The Source's 100 Best Rap Albums in 1998, it remains one of the most impactful debut EPs in hip-hop history.
Gotta give it on up to the Glock-glock, Pop-pop, better drop when them buckshot blow.

Bizzy Bone's genre-defying rap rock album released through Sumerian Records, home to acts like August Burns Red and Asking Alexandria. A bold creative departure that blended his signature rapid-fire delivery with rock production, featuring guest appearances and production from artists far outside his usual musical sphere. Received positive feedback from both hip hop and rock fans.
I get the flippin in fucking them up, If Lil Layzie tell me shoot em I'm shootin them up.

Dirty Paper, Javier Reyes & Ash Avildsen

A French collaboration album between Bizzy Bone and French rapper Papillon Bandana, released before Crossroads: 2010. One of the most unique entries in Bizzy's catalog — a transatlantic project blending American rapid-fire hip hop with French urban music. Features Layzie Bone, Fingazz and Big Sloan. Rated 3.09 out of 5 on Rate Your Music with 33 ratings.
I want you to fill your gas tanks up, halfway empty, halfway full.
So High (Lève ton verre en l'air)

Four months after losing their mentor Eazy-E to AIDS, Bone Thugs-n-Harmony returned with an album that would define them forever. Dedicated to his memory, E. 1999 Eternal debuted at number one on the Billboard 200, sold over four million copies in the United States, and spawned 'Tha Crossroads,' one of the most emotionally charged singles in hip-hop history. Produced entirely by DJ U-Neek, whose dark, smoked-out G-funk beats and brooding synth melodies provided the perfect canvas, the album captured the group at their most focused and cohesive. Critics praised their lightning-fast melodic flows delivered in near-perfect unison, a sound that bordered on singing without ever losing its street-hardened edge. Easily one of the most significant rap albums of the 1990s.
Runnin' through the alley and into the mêlée, Up on the second the sundown, Those run from January, November, December, I'm lovin' the first of the month.

Released in 2006 on Mob Life Records, initially as an internet release before receiving a physical CD pressing. Produced entirely by Hella Tight, the album runs 13 tracks and features guests from Bizzy's 7th Sign circle. Lyrically it sits at the crossroads of street rap and syncretic spirituality, blending Christian and Islamic references with autobiographical reflection. Bizzy Bone has later described himself as a follower of Bay’ah, which he characterizes as a blend of different belief systems and religions. One of four solo albums Bizzy released in 2006, it represents the original, unaltered version of a body of work later repackaged multiple times.

You Don't Really Wanna Be Me

A limited-edition re-release of Evolution of Elevation (2006) featuring the original unaltered versions of the tracks, before Mob Life Records modified the production. The record also features a couple of tracks not included on the earlier edition, including solo cuts from 7th Sign affiliates. A very small pressing making it one of the rarest items in Bizzy Bone's catalog. A must-have for hardcore collectors.
Oooh they don´t knwow, Listen to the shotgun go blow!

Before the platinum records and the Grammy, there was this: a raw debut from five teenagers out of Cleveland, released on a small independent label run by a local record store owner. Recorded under the name B.O.N.E. Enterpri$e, the album introduced what would become one of hip-hop's most distinctive sounds, rapid-fire flows woven into layered vocal harmonies, blurring the line between rapping and singing in a way nobody had heard before. The story of how it led to their big break is the stuff of legend: Krayzie Bone recited a verse over the phone from their 1991 local radio single 'Flow Motion,' impressing Eazy-E enough to invite four of the five members to audition in person, ultimately landing them a deal with Ruthless Records and changing hip-hop history.
I hope you get smoked. I put a gauge to your throat, and it is murder Bizz wrote.

A limited mail-order release of only 1000 copies, made exclusively for Bizzy Bone's dedicated fanbase. One of the rarest physical releases in his catalog, a direct-to-fan project that bypassed traditional distribution entirely. A raw and personal snapshot of Bizzy's creative output during his most prolific independent period. The opening track “Priceless” stands out as a heartfelt tribute to his own family.
I guess the twist of this tongues, A blessin' from the Father, I've been disinfected, wiped clean go ask the barber.

Bizzy Bone's solo debut exploded onto the charts at #3 on the Billboard 200, selling 130,000 copies in its first week. A raw, eclectic take on Bone Thugs-N-Harmony's R&B-laced rap formula with deep autobiography and fire-and-brimstone spirituality. The album includes some of Bizzy Bone's most iconic tracks to date, including “Thugs Cry“ and “Nobody Can Stop Me“, while other tracks “Marchin' on Washington“, “Roll Call“, “Yes Yes Y'all“ and “Brain on Drugs“ were cut short due to sample clearances not being made on time. Produced in part by Bizzy himself under the alias B.B. Gambini, alongside Johnny 'J'. Certified Gold by the RIAA.
Talk to the walkin' dead, crossroads, call on the Rev., lost souls.

Bizzy's fourth album in four years, released on his birthday September 12, 2022. The album doesn't contain appearances from any Bone Thugs-N-Harmony member but highlights his two sons, Lil Bizzy and YBL Sinatra, who serve as the only features throughout. The project arrived amid manufactured controversy: lead single 'Bizzy Gets Busy' seemed to call out his groupmates for excluding him from tours, a tension Bizzy later admitted was a deliberate marketing strategy. The plan worked and the album hit number one on iTunes. Spanning 18 tracks over 58 minutes, it balances dark, spiritually charged introspection with hardcore street energy, showcasing his sons as emerging voices alongside his own.
The alpha beta man is just a mumble rap, And I tend to use bigger words.

Bizzy Bone’s 2011 album Mr. Ouija is a tight, 10-track release issued independently through the Siccness.Net label. Named after one of Bone Thugs-n-Harmony’s most recognizable supernatural themes, the Ouija board’s spirit guide. The album consists of reworked versions of songs from the album Ruthless, which was released through the same label three years earlier.

A duo album by Bizzy Bone and Krayzie Bone under the name Bone Thugs, long anticipated and widely discussed online for years prior to its release. In addition to appearances from other members of Bone Thugs-N-Harmony, the album features a broader guest list than most group-related projects, including Stephen Marley, Bun B, Uncle Murda, and Yelawolf.

A rare limited-press release of only 1000 copies, a companion piece to For the Fans Vol. 1 from 2005, continuing Bizzy's direct-to-fan approach before traditional album distribution. Consists of productions and collaborations with lesser-known artists, reflecting a turbulent period in Bizzy’s life associated with the widely discussed radio interview on Houston station KPFT FM. One of the rarest physical releases in his solo catalog.

Bizzy Bone’s first solo album released through Siccness.net, named after the legendary label that launched Bone Thugs-N-Harmony's career. Features guest appearances from Rick Ross and Pitbull. The entire album was recorded in Nick Cannon‘s studio “Can I Ball”. Material from these recording sessions would later be reused on other releases from the same label. Siccness.net also released a DVD including videos for nine of the album's tracks.
And whether I'm right or wrong, slightly still tipsy, Gypsies in titan songs.

Named after a notorious 2005 incident where Bizzy Bone broke into a spiritual rant live on Houston radio station KPFT FM. Critics praised the album's gritty lo-fi recording quality and Bizzy's spiritually unhinged lyricism. One of his most raw and personal solo efforts, dealing with addiction, faith and redemption.

Still Creepin on Ah Come Up

A collaborative album by Bizzy Bone and Layzie Bone released under the Bone Thugs banner, continuing their Bone Brothers partnership under a new name. A nod to the original 1994 classic that launched their career. Featuring both members' signature rapid harmonics across 50 minutes of material. “Conspiracy“ questions the circumstances surrounding Eazy-E's rapid decline and death, but stands as much as a tribute to their late mentor as it does an accusation. The album received positive reviews from die-hard fans.
Hit 'em with the rhythm like we did it, like back in the day-day.
Still Creepin' On Ah Come Up

Released on his 47th birthday, September 12, 2023, this entirely self-produced album on the JAM tv label is Bizzy's most solitary creative statement, with YBL Sinatra as the sole feature. Across 19 tracks the album covers familiar ground, street life, spirituality and social commentary, but several cuts stand out for their specificity. 'Hopi Tribe' draws on Bizzy's Native American heritage, rooted in time spent on a reservation during his traumatic childhood. 'Rip Pat Stay' is a tribute to Canadian battle rap legend Pat Stay, fatally stabbed in September 2022. The closing track 'BTNH Way' directly celebrates the group's East 99th Street renaming ceremony in Cleveland, which had taken place exactly one month earlier.
Lil ripsta the scepter in my hand and the scripture, Abyss has sent your kisses back, I've done ran all out of liquor.

Bone Thugs-n-Harmony's most ambitious statement, this sprawling double album debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 and was certified four times platinum by the RIAA. Produced entirely by DJ U-Neek, it arrived at a turbulent moment, with Bizzy Bone's personal struggles keeping him away from much of the promotion, while new Ruthless Records leadership Tomica Wright reshaped several tracks against the group's wishes. Yet the album delivered, anchored by 'Look into My Eyes,' which peaked at number four on the Billboard Hot 100, and a posthumous collaboration with 2Pac on 'Thug Luv,' a verse Bizzy confirmed Pac wrote in under two minutes. Krayzie Bone has since called it the group's finest work, crediting the entirely fresh, studio-born material as what sets it apart from its legendary predecessor.
I shedded my last tear when I found out love was a lie.
If I Could Teach the World
How Many of Us Have Them (Friends)

One of the rarest and most overlooked releases in Bizzy Bone's catalog, this self-produced, self-released limited-edition CD came about as a direct response to the fan reception of Alpha and Omega (2004). Released on his own 7 Sign Record label and sold directly to fans through early internet distribution, it bypasses the traditional music industry entirely, an unusually bold move for its time. Featuring his tight-knit 7th Sign Regime collective, including Layzie Bone on 'Head to the Ground,' the album is a raw, unfiltered document of Bizzy's independent spirit during one of the most turbulent periods of his life. Lyrically it covers familiar ground, street life, spirituality, and personal introspection, delivered with the intensity of an artist with nothing to prove and no label to answer to. For those who have heard it, this is an underground gem that deserves far wider recognition, and physical copies are genuinely scarce, regularly changing hands for $50–$130 on the secondary market.
Nobody wants to sidekick, Cool when they first met you, Now they want the respect that I get.
Big B, Weezy, Prince Rasu & Capo

Released on his 49th birthday, September 12, 2025, this is Bizzy's most ambitious independent project in scope if not in sound. The music spans 17 tracks across just under 40 minutes, entirely self-produced with no guest features, blending his signature rapid-fire chopper flow with raw lyricism and soulful melodic passages. What sets it apart is the surrounding multimedia package: a DVD and a comic book titled 'Cleveland Before the Harmony' are sold alongside the CD, suggesting Bizzy has conceived this not merely as an album but as the foundation of a broader narrative world rooted in Cleveland's history before Bone Thugs-N-Harmony existed. A limited hoodie from an accompanying animated series sold out immediately.
Non of that Word salad, everything i speak to you is word valid.

Considered by many fans to be Bizzy's finest solo work. A deeply confessional album covering childhood trauma, the loss of Eazy-E and spiritual identity. Peaked at #44 on Billboard 200 and #2 on Independent Albums. The track “Fried Day,” which was also featured on the Next Friday soundtrack, is Bizzy Bone’s most-streamed song on Spotify, with over 20 million streams.
Cleveland, the city we come from, Redrum, And murda mo I can feel some.

A boldly titled 15-track album released through Thump Records — notably without Bizzy Bone's knowledge or endorsement, as DJ U-Neek released it without paying him. Despite the controversy, the album is a solid entry in his catalog featuring West Coast g-funk flavors and rapid-fire delivery across subjects from street life to religion.

Produced entirely by Blais, this solo statement is widely seen as one of his stronger later-career releases. True to his traditional melodic, uptempo style, it creates what sounds like a missing entry in the larger Bone Thugs-N-Harmony discography, while also making a noticeable effort to weave in something more contemporary. A purely self-contained project with Bizzy as sole executive producer.
I ain't no motherfuckin model, I don't walk the runway neither, Imma pull out pistol, after pistol, Until the police see em.
Fake Love - I Am Not Them

The third of four albums Bizzy Bone released in 2006, distributed via Koch Entertainment. Production handled entirely by Playalitical with a diverse sound ranging from g-funk to synth melodies. Features guest appearances from Young Droop, Playalitical and Spoke-In-Wordz. Considered by many fans to be one of his stronger independent releases.
And one for the love of the Lord and Heavenly Father we need us a sword, But we know no weapons are formed.

Originally recorded as From Your Righteousness (F.Y.R.) around 2006–2007 and first released in 2008, this 13-track independent project captures Bizzy Bone at the peak of his Islamic spiritual exploration. No major label backed the project. Bizzy handled the release independently, featuring only his tight-knit 7th Sign collective, Rasu, Josiah, and Aeileon, as collaborators, with no outside guest appearances. The album never charted on Billboard, received zero professional reviews, and was distributed through CD Baby, yet a small core of dedicated fans considers it among his most heartfelt and focused work.
Working for the Lord is a blessing for me. I am a servant, and I try to travel righteously.

Released during one of the most turbulent periods of his career, estranged from Bone Thugs-N-Harmony and battling personal demons, Bizzy channeled his isolation into a remarkably focused solo statement. Produced by Big Hollis, Tron Treez, and Vince V., the album features no guest artists whatsoever, a deliberate choice that gives it the feel of an unfiltered confessional. Lyrically it moves through autobiography, defiance, and vulnerability in equal measure. Remarkably, it arrived just 21 days after Thugs Revenge, making it the second of four solo albums Bizzy released in 2006 alone.
Bryon, Orion my vision I'm here to unite the fuckin aliens and angels.

Released through Krayzie Bone’s label The Life Apparel. A more laid-back and melodic effort compared to his rapid-fire earlier work, showing his range as an artist. The album opens with a radio voice referencing a UFO incident (Varginha, 1996), a recurring theme throughout Bizzy Bone’s career. The female rapper Dutchess appears as the only guest artist. The album artwork contains no production credits, while Bizzy himself is listed as executive producer.
Calling my chauffer, Please get off my sofa

A collaboration with Long Beach rapper Bad Azz, a West Coast legend affiliated with DPGC, which also inspired the album’s title (Bone Thugs + Dogg Pound). The collaboration was preceded by earlier joint efforts on Bizzy’s releases through Hi Power Entertainment. Like Back With The Thugs Vol. 2, the album was produced by Seriouss Sam, Makavelik, and Nu-Ration, though individual track credits are not specified. Bad Azz passed away on November 11, 2019, under unclear circumstances, four days after being arrested and jailed for domestic violence.
This is for tranquility, heaven screaming out bloody hell, Bail me out quickly.

The fifth studio album by Bone Thugs-N-Harmony and their final release for Ruthless Records, not counting the later compilation T.H.U.G.S. Features the singles “Money Money“, “Get Up & Get It“ and “Home“ featuring Phil Collins. The latter charted in the UK, earning him the honorary moniker “Chrome Bone“. Flesh-n-Bone was absent due to incarceration. Bizzy Bone was ejected from the group shortly after the album's release.
I'mma be a soldier, Runnin' from gun shots for the day I see Jehovah.

A Ruthless Records cash-grab compilation of previously unreleased vault tracks that hadn't made the cut of BTNHResurrection and Thug World Order, largely remixed with new beats. Features Bizzy, Krayzie, Layzie and Wish Bone (Flesh absent). Despite the controversy, songs like 'Everyday Thugs' and 'Don't Waste My Time' are fan favorites. Debuted at #73 on the Billboard 200.
Been around the hood and now you're motherfuckin eyed, Rather die than to be caught up in deception and lies, word to the wise.

Released on February 28, 2006, via Hi Power Entertainment, Thugs Revenge is a 16-track gangsta rap album that marries Bizzy Bone's signature chopper flow with West Coast Chicano rap production. The project was one out of four solo releases Bizzy dropped that year alone, during a turbulent period of estrangement from Bone Thugs-N-Harmony, from which he had been expelled in 2003. The album remains beloved by some devoted fans as a return to his hardcore roots. The Mr. Criminal–dominated production gave Bizzy a polished, G-Funk–inflected canvas distinct from anything else in his catalog.
Everybody on the West Side, My papito, mamacita in the barrio, Tequila my amigo.

Released September 11, 2007 on Real Talk Entertainment/Koch Records, this was the retail-ready reworking of the same sessions behind Evolution of Elevation. Roughly half the tracks received new production from Vince V. and Big Hollis, while all song titles were changed and one additional track added. The album sits at the crossroads of street rap, gospel rap, and syncretic spirituality, with spiritual references shifting between Christian and Islamic frameworks.
Rocks are bigger than boulders, Whether it's Colorado or if it's California

The first album featuring all five original members since BTNHResurrection a decade earlier, Uni5: The World's Enemy was a genuinely historic reunion. Getting all five of these large and turbulent characters into the studio together for a whole album was no small feat: Flesh-N-Bone had spent a decade in prison, Bizzy had been ousted from the group, and the intervening years had been marked by legal trouble and internal conflict. Released May 4, 2010, on their own BTNH Worldwide label distributed by Warner Bros., the album debuted at number 14 on the Billboard 200, selling 30,000 copies in its first week and over 90,000 copies in total. Production came from a team including Krayzie Bone, Thin C, Excel Beats, and Pooh Bear, leaning toward a more polished, contemporary sound than the group's classic work. Critics noted Bizzy's contributions as a particular highlight, lighting up every track he appeared on with his biblical perspective and unpredictable energy.
We're ready for anything and we sing to make through it.
A New Mind = A New Life (Interlude)

Self-released on his 45th birthday as part of an annual September 12 tradition, this is widely regarded by fans as Bizzy's most cohesive and focused solo album since Heaven'z Movie back in 1998. Produced by C-Lance and a tight circle of collaborators, it channels the sinister, rapid-fire aesthetic of classic Bone Thugs with dark piano melodies, atmospheric beats, and Bizzy's trademark chopper flow deployed at full force. Lyrically it circles mortality, demons, and defiance throughout. Three months after its release, the infamous Verzuz brawl with Three 6 Mafia put Bizzy back in the spotlight nationally. Fun fact: Apart from Lil Bizzy and YBL Sinatra, the album artwork credits special guest appearances from Lil Ripsta, BB Gambini and Rest In Peace, which are all aliases used by Bizzy Bone himsel
Enemy opps, Feeling me Glocks, Finna be shots.

Released eight months after the original, this expanded version adds seven new tracks and a full remaster of all 14 originals, with the track sequence completely rearranged rather than simply appended. Released under the JAM tv imprint, named after his family's YouTube channel, the Deluxe Edition underscores how deeply personal this creative phase had become for Bizzy. For fans, it deepens an already strong project into something that stands as one of the highlights of his independent late-career output.
These niggas is hoes with the pistol in they purses,I put my pistol under my clothes like im supposed too.
